title: secretx-core: remove put from SecretStore, add WriteableSecretStore, generic get_blocking
description:
What: Foundation change — remove put from SecretStore, define WriteableSecretStore supertrait, make get_blocking generic.

Algorithm:
1. In secretx-core/src/lib.rs, remove 'async fn put(&self, value: SecretValue) -> Result<(), SecretError>' from the SecretStore trait body.
2. Below the SecretStore definition, add:
   '#[async_trait::async_trait]
   pub trait WriteableSecretStore: SecretStore {
       async fn put(&self, value: SecretValue) -> Result<(), SecretError>;
   }'
3. Change get_blocking signature from 'pub fn get_blocking(store: &dyn SecretStore)' to 'pub fn get_blocking<S: SecretStore + ?Sized>(store: &S)'. The body is unchanged.
4. In secretx-core/src/lib.rs tests, both FakeStore structs implement SecretStore with a put body. Remove put from their 'impl SecretStore for FakeStore' blocks. Add 'impl WriteableSecretStore for FakeStore { async fn put... }' blocks for each, since the get_blocking tests need put to exist on FakeStore.
5. Add WriteableSecretStore to the public re-exports list (if any exist at this level — secretx-core exports everything it defines so no change needed; all items are pub).
6. Run 'cargo test -p secretx-core' and confirm all tests pass.

Defensive rules:
- #[async_trait] must annotate BOTH the WriteableSecretStore trait definition AND every impl block for it.
- WriteableSecretStore must not re-declare Send+Sync — it inherits them from SecretStore.
- get_blocking must keep the ?Sized bound so dyn SecretStore remains usable.
- Do not add any unwrap() calls.

Tests: existing tests in secretx-core must all pass. The two FakeStore impls in tests must compile with WriteableSecretStore.
